1.19: They were not one of us , because they were not sincerely Christian. Christians only by baptism, unbelievers by the perversity of their doctrine and conduct.
1.2: Every mind , etc. This is not to say that the confession of this point of faith alone is sufficient at all times and in all cases; but it relates to that time and to that part of Christian doctrine which had to be particularly confessed, taught and maintained against the heretics who had appeared; it was the best mark by which one could distinguish the true from the false doctors.
1.4: Everything that is born of God, Romans
1.16: Who does not go to their death , which does not lead to final impenitence, which causes eternal death to the soul. ― This is not , etc. Saint John does not forbid praying for those who commit such a sin; for there is no absolutely unforgivable sin, but he does not dare to give the faithful the confidence of being heard for this one, a confidence he inspired in them with regard to all others. The sin that leads to death , which leads to spiritual death, which breaks all communion of life with the Son of God, is apostasy or hardening of the heart. It's not for that sin. , etc. Saint John does not forbid praying for apostates; nor does he say that such prayers would never be answered. But he observes that the preceding recommendation concerns other sinners, and he suggests that prayer for apo
1.8: . Clever! , of the demon. ― Cain… killed his brother Abel.
1.18: There charity perfect, or love, chases away fear of men, as well as any anxiety that leads us to doubt God's mercy, and that servile fear that makes us dread the punishment of sin rather than God's offense. But it does not exclude the salutary fear of God's judgments, so often recommended in the biblical books, any more than that fear and trembling with which Saint Paul (see Philippines , (
1.6: Do not sin ; That is to say, he does not fall into grave sins, he does not give in to crime; if he commits some fault through weakness, he takes care to expiate them through penance.
1.44: . appeared into the world, came into the world.
1.17: for such is Jesus Christ . Since Jesus Christ is holy and spotless, we too must keep ourselves in this world pure from all stain of sin.
1.3: Who does not confess this Jesus? , either by denying his human nature, or his divinity, or by denying that he is the promised Messiah sent by God.

2.1: My grandchildren, I am writing these things to you so that you may not sin. And if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous one.
2.2: He is himself a propitiatory victim for our sins, and not only for ours, but for those of the whole world.
2.3: And this is how we know that we have come to know him: if we keep his commandments.
2.4: He who says he knows Him and does not keep His commandments is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
2.5: But whoever keeps his word, in him the love of God is truly perfected; by this we know that we are in him.
2.6: He who says he abides in him must also walk as he himself walked.
2.7: My beloved, I am not writing you a new commandment, but an old commandment which you have received from the beginning; this old commandment is the word which you have heard.
2.8: On the other hand, I am writing you a new commandment, which has been verified in Christ Jesus and in you, because the darkness is passing away and the true light is already shining.
2.9: He who claims to be in the light and yet hates his brother is still in darkness.
2.10: He who loves his brother abides in the light, and there is nothing in him to sin.
2.11: But he who hates his brother is in the darkness. He walks in the darkness, not knowing where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
2.12: I am writing to you, little children, because your sins have been forgiven you for his name's sake.
2.13: I am writing to you, fathers, because you have known him who is from the beginning. I am writing to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one.
2.14: I have written to you, dear children, because you have known the Father. I have written to you, fathers, because you have known him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong and the word of God abides in you and you have overcome the evil one.
2.15: Do not love the world or anything in the world. If anyone loves the world, love for the Father is not in them.
2.16: For everything in the world—the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life—comes not from the Father but from the world.
2.17: The world passes away, and so do its desires, but whoever does the will of God lives forever.
2.18: My grandchildren, this is the last hour. As you have heard that the antichrist is coming, even now there are many antichrists: this is how we know it is the last hour.
2.19: They went out from among us, but they were not of us, for if they had been of us, they would have remained with us; but they went out, so that it might be manifest that not all of them were of us.
2.20: For you, it is from the Holy One that you have received the anointing and you know everything.
2.21: I have written to you, not because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it and you know that no lie comes from the truth.
2.22: Who is the liar, if not the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? That one is the antichrist, who denies the Father and the Son.
2.23: Whoever denies the Son does not have the Father either; whoever acknowledges the Son has the Father also.
2.24: For you, let what you have heard from the beginning remain in you. If what you have heard from the beginning remains in you, you also remain in the Son and in the Father.
2.25: And the promise that he himself made to us is eternal life.
2.26: That's what I had to write to you about those who seduce you.
2.27: As for you, the anointing you received from him remains in you, and you do not need anyone to teach you, but as his anointing teaches you about all things, this teaching is true and not a lie; and as it has taught you, remain in him.
2.28: And now, my grandchildren, remain in him, so that when he appears we may have confidence and not be cast away from him in shame at his coming.
2.29: If you know that he is just, recognize that whoever practices righteousness is born of him.

3.1: See what great love the Father has lavished on us, that we should be called children of God! And that is what we are! The reason the world does not know us is that it did not know him.
3.2: My beloved, we are now children of God, and what we will be has not yet been revealed, but we know that at the time of that revelation we shall be like him because we shall see him as he is.
3.3: Whoever has this hope in him purifies himself, just as he is pure.
3.4: Whoever commits sin breaks the law, and sin is the transgression of the law.
3.5: But you know that Jesus appeared to take away sins, and that sin is not in him.
3.6: Whoever remains in him does not sin; whoever sins has neither seen him nor known him.
3.7: Little children, let no one deceive you. He who practices righteousness is righteous, just as he is righteous.
3.8: The one who commits sin is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the works of the devil.
3.9: Anyone who is born of God does not commit sin, because God's seed remains in them; they cannot sin, because they are born of God.
3.10: This is how we know who the children of God are and who the children of the devil are. Anyone who does not do what is right is not a child of God, nor is anyone who does not love their brother or sister.
3.11: For the message you heard from the beginning is that we should love one another,
3.12: not like Cain, who belonged to the evil one and killed his brother. And why did he kill him? Because his own deeds were evil, while his brother's were righteous.
3.13: Don't be surprised, my brothers, if the world hates you.
3.14: We know that we have passed from death to life because we love our brothers and sisters. Whoever does not love remains in death.
3.15: Anyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
3.16: This is how we know what love is: He laid down his life for us. And we too must lay down our lives for our brothers and sisters.
3.17: If someone has the goods of this world and sees his brother in need, yet closes his heart to them, how does the love of God abide in him?
3.18: My grandchildren, let us not love in words and speech, but in deeds and in truth.
3.19: By this we know that we belong to the truth, and we can reassure our hearts before God.,
3.20: For if our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and he knows all things.
3.21: My beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we can approach God with confidence.
3.22: Whatever we ask for, we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do what is pleasing in his sight.
3.23: And his commandment is that we believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and that we love one another, as he has commanded us.
3.24: He who keeps his commandments abides in God, and God in him, and we know that he abides in us by the Spirit whom he has given us.

4.1: My beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test whether the spirits are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world.
4.2: This is how you will recognize the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God,
4.3: and every spirit that does not confess this Jesus is not from God: it is the spirit of the antichrist, whose coming you have been told about, and even now is already in the world.
4.4: You, my little children, are from God and have overcome them, because the one who is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
4.5: They are of the world, that is why they speak the language of the world and the world listens to them.
4.6: But we are from God, and whoever knows God listens to us; whoever is not from God does not listen to us. This is how we recognize the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
4.7: My beloved, let us love one another, for love comes from God, and whoever loves has been born of God and knows God.
4.8: Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love.
4.9: He demonstrated his love for us by sending his only Son into the world, so that we might live through him.
4.10: And this is love: not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son as an atoning sacrifice for our sins.
4.11: My beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
4.12: No one has ever seen God, but if we love one another, God abides in us, and his love is perfected in us.
4.13: We know that we abide in him and he abides in us, in that he gives us of his Spirit.
4.14: And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent us the Son as Savior of the world.
4.15: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him and he in God.
4.16: And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.
4.17: The perfection of love in us is that we have sure confidence on the day of judgment, for as Jesus Christ is, so are we in this world.
4.18: There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear, because fear involves punishment, and whoever fears is not perfect in love.
4.19: Therefore, we love God because God first loved us.
4.20: If anyone says, «I love God,» and hates his brother, he is a liar; how can someone who does not love his brother whom he has seen love God whom he has not seen?
4.21: And we have received this commandment from him: «Whoever loves God must also love his brother.»

5.1: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ is born of God, and everyone who loves the Father loves his child as well.
5.2: By this mark we know that we love the children of God, if we love God and if we observe his commandments.
5.3: For keeping God's commandments is love. And his commandments are not burdensome.
5.4: because everyone born of God overcomes the world, and this is the victory that has overcome the world, even our faith.
5.5: Who is it that overcomes the world, except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
5.6: This is the same Jesus Christ who came by water and blood; not by water only, but by water and blood. And it is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.
5.7: For there are three that bear witness [in heaven: the Father, the Word and the Spirit, and these three are one.
5.8: And there are three that testify on earth: the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and these three agree.
5.9: If we accept the testimony of men, the testimony of God is greater, and this is indeed the testimony of God, who has borne witness to his Son.
5.10: Whoever believes in the Son of God has this testimony (of God) in himself; whoever does not believe God makes him a liar, because he has not believed the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.
5.11: And this is the testimony: God has given us eternal life, and that this life is in his Son.
5.12: Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
5.13: I have written these things to you, so that you may know that you have eternal life, you who believe in the name of the Son of God.
5.14: And we have this full confidence in God, that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
5.15: And if we know that he listens to us, whatever we ask of him, we know that we get what we asked for.
5.16: If anyone sees their brother or sister commit a sin that does not lead to death, they should pray, and God will give that person life. There is a sin that does lead to death; I am not saying that you should pray about that.
5.17: All iniquity is a sin, and there are some sins that do not lead to death.
5.18: We know that anyone born of God does not sin, but the one who was born of God keeps himself safe, and the evil one does not harm him.
5.19: We know that we are of God and that the whole world is steeped in evil.
5.20: But we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know him who is true. And we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. He is the true God and eternal life.
